name: manual_e3ec78
claim: Before trusting a commit's scope from its title, run 'git show --stat <sha>' — the title states intent, not actual changes. A small-sounding title with stats showing 1000+ line changes or touching files outside the title's scope signals a stealth refactor.

decisions.notes: Commit b99796f on this repo was titled 'feat: sticky mobile booking bar' but its stat showed styles.css ±2530 lines and a 132-line index.html restructure — it was actually a full LUXE redesign merge that deleted the .animate-in CSS rule, leaving the site shipping with all 12 sections permanently invisible (opacity:0) for ~2 months. Nobody caught it because everyone trusted the commit title. The stat line would have flagged it in 5 seconds.

rationale: Commit b99796f on this repo was titled 'feat: sticky mobile booking bar' but its stat showed styles.css ±2530 lines and a 132-line index.html restructure — it was actually a full LUXE redesign merge that deleted the .animate-in CSS rule, leaving the site shipping with all 12 sections permanently invisible (opacity:0) for ~2 months. Nobody caught it because everyone trusted the commit title. The stat line would have flagged it in 5 seconds.
